resolvedSkills.description: Example ClawFlow authoring pattern for inbox triage. Use when messages need different treatment based on intent, with some routes notifying immediately, some waiting on outside answers, and others rolling into a later summary.

resolvedSkills.name: clawhub
resolvedSkills.description: Use the ClawHub CLI to search, install, update, and publish agent skills from clawhub.com. Use when you need to fetch new skills on the fly, sync installed skills to latest or a specific version, or publish new/updated skill folders with the npm-installed clawhub CLI.

resolvedSkills.name: coding-agent
resolvedSkills.description: Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Claude Code: use --print --permission-mode bypassPermissions (no PTY). Codex/Pi/OpenCode: pty:true required.

resolvedSkills.name: eightctl
resolvedSkills.description: Control Eight Sleep pods (status, temperature, alarms, schedules).

resolvedSkills.name: gemini
resolvedSkills.description: Gemini CLI for one-shot Q&A, summaries, and generation.

resolvedSkills.name: gh-issues
resolvedSkills.description: Fetch GitHub issues, spawn sub-agents to implement fixes and open PRs, then monitor and address PR review comments. Usage: /gh-issues [owner/repo] [--label bug] [--limit 5] [--milestone v1.0] [--assignee @me] [--fork user/repo] [--watch] [--interval 5] [--reviews-only] [--cron] [--dry-run] [--model glm-5] [--notify-channel -1002381931352]

resolvedSkills.name: gifgrep
resolvedSkills.description: Search GIF providers with CLI/TUI, download results, and extract stills/sheets.

resolvedSkills.name: github
resolvedSkills.description: GitHub operations via `gh` CLI: issues, PRs, CI runs, code review, API queries. Use when: (1) checking PR status or CI, (2) creating/commenting on issues, (3) listing/filtering PRs or issues, (4) viewing run logs. NOT for: complex web UI interactions requiring manual browser flows (use browser tooling when available), bulk operations across many repos (script with gh api), or when gh auth is not configured.

resolvedSkills.name: gog
resolvedSkills.description: Google Workspace CLI for Gmail, Calendar, Drive, Contacts, Sheets, and Docs.

resolvedSkills.name: healthcheck
resolvedSkills.description: Host security hardening and risk-tolerance configuration for OpenClaw deployments. Use when a user asks for security audits, firewall/SSH/update hardening, risk posture, exposure review, OpenClaw cron scheduling for periodic checks, or version status checks on a machine running OpenClaw (laptop, workstation, Pi, VPS).

resolvedSkills.name: himalaya
resolvedSkills.description: CLI to manage emails via IMAP/SMTP. Use `himalaya` to list, read, write, reply, forward, search, and organize emails from the terminal. Supports multiple accounts and message composition with MML (MIME Meta Language).

resolvedSkills.name: imsg
resolvedSkills.description: iMessage/SMS CLI for listing chats, history, and sending messages via Messages.app.

resolvedSkills.name: mcporter
resolvedSkills.description: Use the mcporter CLI to list, configure, auth, and call MCP servers/tools directly (HTTP or stdio), including ad-hoc servers, config edits, and CLI/type generation.

resolvedSkills.name: nano-pdf
resolvedSkills.description: Edit PDFs with natural-language instructions using the nano-pdf CLI.

resolvedSkills.name: node-connect
resolvedSkills.description: Diagnose OpenClaw node connection and pairing failures for Android, iOS, and macOS companion apps. Use when QR/setup code/manual connect fails, local Wi-Fi works but VPS/tailnet does not, or errors mention pairing required, unauthorized, bootstrap token invalid or expired, gateway.bind, gateway.remote.url, Tailscale, or plugins.entries.device-pair.config.publicUrl.

resolvedSkills.name: obsidian
resolvedSkills.description: Work with Obsidian vaults (plain Markdown notes) and automate via obsidian-cli.

resolvedSkills.name: openhue
resolvedSkills.description: Control Philips Hue lights and scenes via the OpenHue CLI.

resolvedSkills.name: oracle
resolvedSkills.description: Best practices for using the oracle CLI (prompt + file bundling, engines, sessions, and file attachment patterns).

resolvedSkills.name: ordercli
resolvedSkills.description: Foodora-only CLI for checking past orders and active order status (Deliveroo WIP).

resolvedSkills.name: peekaboo
resolvedSkills.description: Capture and automate macOS UI with the Peekaboo CLI.

resolvedSkills.name: skill-creator
resolvedSkills.description: Create, edit, improve, or audit AgentSkills. Use when creating a new skill from scratch or when asked to improve, review, audit, tidy up, or clean up an existing skill or SKILL.md file. Also use when editing or restructuring a skill directory (moving files to references/ or scripts/, removing stale content, validating against the AgentSkills spec). Triggers on phrases like "create a skill", "author a skill", "tidy up a skill", "improve this skill", "review the skill", "clean up the skill", "audit the skill".

resolvedSkills.name: slack
resolvedSkills.description: Use when you need to control Slack from OpenClaw via the slack tool, including reacting to messages or pinning/unpinning items in Slack channels or DMs.

resolvedSkills.name: songsee
resolvedSkills.description: Generate spectrograms and feature-panel visualizations from audio with the songsee CLI.

resolvedSkills.name: sonoscli
resolvedSkills.description: Control Sonos speakers (discover/status/play/volume/group).

resolvedSkills.name: summarize
resolvedSkills.description: Summarize or extract text/transcripts from URLs, podcasts, and local files (great fallback for “transcribe this YouTube/video”).

resolvedSkills.name: things-mac
resolvedSkills.description: Manage Things 3 via the `things` CLI on macOS (add/update projects+todos via URL scheme; read/search/list from the local Things database). Use when a user asks OpenClaw to add a task to Things, list inbox/today/upcoming, search tasks, or inspect projects/areas/tags.

resolvedSkills.name: video-frames
resolvedSkills.description: Extract frames or short clips from videos using ffmpeg.

resolvedSkills.name: wacli

resolvedSkills.name: weather
resolvedSkills.description: Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.

Use the read tool to load a skill's file when the task matches its description.

resolvedSkills.description: Generate spectrograms and feature-panel visualizations from audio with the songsee CLI.

resolvedSkills.name: sonoscli
resolvedSkills.description: Control Sonos speakers (discover/status/play/volume/group).

resolvedSkills.name: summarize
resolvedSkills.description: Summarize or extract text/transcripts from URLs, podcasts, and local files (great fallback for “transcribe this YouTube/video”).

resolvedSkills.name: things-mac
resolvedSkills.description: Manage Things 3 via the `things` CLI on macOS (add/update projects+todos via URL scheme; read/search/list from the local Things database). Use when a user asks OpenClaw to add a task to Things, list inbox/today/upcoming, search tasks, or inspect projects/areas/tags.

resolvedSkills.name: video-frames
resolvedSkills.description: Extract frames or short clips from videos using ffmpeg.

resolvedSkills.name: wacli
resolvedSkills.description: Send WhatsApp messages to other people or search/sync WhatsApp history via the wacli CLI (not for normal user chats).

resolvedSkills.name: weather
resolvedSkills.description: Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.

skillsSnapshot.prompt:
The following skills provide specialized instructions for specific tasks.
Use the read tool to load a skill's file when the task matches its description.
When a skill file references a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory (parent of SKILL.md / dirname of the path) and use that absolute path in tool commands.

<available_skills>
  <skill>
    <name>1password</name>
    <description>Set up and use 1Password CLI (op). Use when installing the CLI, enabling desktop app integration, signing in (single or multi-account), or reading/injecting/running secrets via op.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/1password/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>apple-notes</name>
    <description>Manage Apple Notes via the `memo` CLI on macOS (create, view, edit, delete, search, move, and export notes). Use when a user asks OpenClaw to add a note, list notes, search notes, or manage note folders.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/apple-notes/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>apple-reminders</name>
    <description>Manage Apple Reminders via remindctl CLI (list, add, edit, complete, delete). Supports lists, date filters, and JSON/plain output.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/apple-reminders/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>bear-notes</name>
    <description>Create, search, and manage Bear notes via grizzly CLI.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/bear-notes/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>blogwatcher</name>
    <description>Monitor blogs and RSS/Atom feeds for updates using the blogwatcher CLI.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/blogwatcher/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>blucli</name>
    <description>BluOS CLI (blu) for discovery, playback, grouping, and volume.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/blucli/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>camsnap</name>
    <description>Capture frames or clips from RTSP/ONVIF cameras.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/camsnap/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>clawflow</name>
    <description>Use when work should span one or more detached tasks but still behave like one job with a single owner context. ClawFlow is the runtime substrate under authoring layers like Lobster, acpx, or plain code. Keep conditional logic in the caller; use ClawFlow for flow identity, waiting, outputs, and user-facing emergence.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/clawflow/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>clawflow-inbox-triage</name>
    <description>Example ClawFlow authoring pattern for inbox triage. Use when messages need different treatment based on intent, with some routes notifying immediately, some waiting on outside answers, and others rolling into a later summary.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/clawflow-inbox-triage/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>clawhub</name>
    <description>Use the ClawHub CLI to search, install, update, and publish agent skills from clawhub.com. Use when you need to fetch new skills on the fly, sync installed skills to latest or a specific version, or publish new/updated skill folders with the npm-installed clawhub CLI.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/clawhub/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>coding-agent</name>
    <description>Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Claude Code: use --print --permission-mode bypassPermissions (no PTY). Codex/Pi/OpenCode: pty:true required.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/coding-agent/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>eightctl</name>
    <description>Control Eight Sleep pods (status, temperature, alarms, schedules).</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/eightctl/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>gemini</name>
    <description>Gemini CLI for one-shot Q&A, summaries, and generation.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/gemini/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>gh-issues</name>
    <description>Fetch GitHub issues, spawn sub-agents to implement fixes and open PRs, then monitor and address PR review comments. Usage: /gh-issues [owner/repo] [--label bug] [--limit 5] [--milestone v1.0] [--assignee @me] [--fork user/repo] [--watch] [--interval 5] [--reviews-only] [--cron] [--dry-run] [--model glm-5] [--notify-channel -1002381931352]</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/gh-issues/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>gifgrep</name>
    <description>Search GIF providers with CLI/TUI, download results, and extract stills/sheets.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/gifgrep/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>github</name>
    <description>GitHub operations via `gh` CLI: issues, PRs, CI runs, code review, API queries. Use when: (1) checking PR status or CI, (2) creating/commenting on issues, (3) listing/filtering PRs or issues, (4) viewing run logs. NOT for: complex web UI interactions requiring manual browser flows (use browser tooling when available), bulk operations across many repos (script with gh api), or when gh auth is not configured.</description>

    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/healthcheck/SKILL.md</location>

resolvedSkills.name: clawflow
resolvedSkills.description: Use when work should span one or more detached tasks but still behave like one job with a single owner context. Keep conditional logic in the caller; use ClawFlow for flow identity, waiting, outputs, and user-facing emergence.

Use the read tool to load a skill's file when the task matches its description.
When a skill file references a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory (parent of SKILL.md / dirname of the path) and use that absolute path in tool commands.

resolvedSkills.name: 1password
resolvedSkills.description: Set up and use 1Password CLI (op). Use when installing the CLI, enabling desktop app integration, signing in (single or multi-account), or reading/injecting/running secrets via op.

resolvedSkills.name: apple-notes
resolvedSkills.description: Manage Apple Notes via the `memo` CLI on macOS (create, view, edit, delete, search, move, and export notes). Use when a user asks OpenClaw to add a note, list notes, search notes, or manage note folders.

resolvedSkills.name: apple-reminders
resolvedSkills.description: Manage Apple Reminders via remindctl CLI (list, add, edit, complete, delete). Supports lists, date filters, and JSON/plain output.

resolvedSkills.name: bear-notes
resolvedSkills.description: Create, search, and manage Bear notes via grizzly CLI.

resolvedSkills.name: blogwatcher
resolvedSkills.description: Monitor blogs and RSS/Atom feeds for updates using the blogwatcher CLI.

resolvedSkills.name: blucli
resolvedSkills.description: BluOS CLI (blu) for discovery, playback, grouping, and volume.

resolvedSkills.name: camsnap
resolvedSkills.description: Capture frames or clips from RTSP/ONVIF cameras.

resolvedSkills.name: clawflow
resolvedSkills.description: Use when work should span one or more detached tasks but still behave like one job with a single owner context. ClawFlow is the runtime substrate under authoring layers like Lobster, acpx, or plain code. Keep conditional logic in the caller; use ClawFlow for flow identity, waiting, outputs, and user-facing emergence.

resolvedSkills.name: clawflow-inbox-triage
resolvedSkills.description: Example ClawFlow authoring pattern for inbox triage. Use when messages need different treatment based on intent, with some routes notifying immediately, some waiting on outside answers, and others rolling into a later summary.

resolvedSkills.name: clawhub
resolvedSkills.description: Use the ClawHub CLI to search, install, update, and publish agent skills from clawhub.com. Use when you need to fetch new skills on the fly, sync installed skills to latest or a specific version, or publish new/updated skill folders with the npm-installed clawhub CLI.

resolvedSkills.name: coding-agent
resolvedSkills.description: Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Claude Code: use --print --permission-mode bypassPermissions (no PTY). Codex/Pi/OpenCode: pty:true required.

resolvedSkills.name: eightctl
resolvedSkills.description: Control Eight Sleep pods (status, temperature, alarms, schedules).

resolvedSkills.name: gemini
resolvedSkills.description: Gemini CLI for one-shot Q&A, summaries, and generation.

resolvedSkills.name: gh-issues
resolvedSkills.description: Fetch GitHub issues, spawn sub-agents to implement fixes and open PRs, then monitor and address PR review comments. Usage: /gh-issues [owner/repo] [--label bug] [--limit 5] [--milestone v1.0] [--assignee @me] [--fork user/repo] [--watch] [--interval 5] [--reviews-only] [--cron] [--dry-run] [--model glm-5] [--notify-channel -1002381931352]

resolvedSkills.name: gifgrep
resolvedSkills.description: Search GIF providers with CLI/TUI, download results, and extract stills/sheets.

resolvedSkills.name: github
resolvedSkills.description: GitHub operations via `gh` CLI: issues, PRs, CI runs, code review, API queries. Use when: (1) checking PR status or CI, (2) creating/commenting on issues, (3) listing/filtering PRs or issues, (4) viewing run logs. NOT for: complex web UI interactions requiring manual browser flows (use browser tooling when available), bulk operations across many repos (script with gh api), or when gh auth is not configured.

resolvedSkills.name: gog
resolvedSkills.description: Google Workspace CLI for Gmail, Calendar, Drive, Contacts, Sheets, and Docs.

resolvedSkills.name: healthcheck
resolvedSkills.description: Host security hardening and risk-tolerance configuration for OpenClaw deployments. Use when a user asks for security audits, firewall/SSH/update hardening, risk posture, exposure review, OpenClaw cron scheduling for periodic checks, or version status checks on a machine running OpenClaw (laptop, workstation, Pi, VPS).

resolvedSkills.name: 1password
resolvedSkills.description: Set up and use 1Password CLI (op). Use when installing the CLI, enabling desktop app integration, signing in (single or multi-account), or reading/injecting/running secrets via op.

resolvedSkills.name: apple-notes
resolvedSkills.description: Manage Apple Notes via the `memo` CLI on macOS (create, view, edit, delete, search, move, and export notes). Use when a user asks OpenClaw to add a note, list notes, search notes, or manage note folders.

resolvedSkills.name: apple-reminders
resolvedSkills.description: Manage Apple Reminders via remindctl CLI (list, add, edit, complete, delete). Supports lists, date filters, and JSON/plain output.

resolvedSkills.name: bear-notes
resolvedSkills.description: Create, search, and manage Bear notes via grizzly CLI.

resolvedSkills.name: blogwatcher
resolvedSkills.description: Monitor blogs and RSS/Atom feeds for updates using the blogwatcher CLI.

resolvedSkills.name: blucli
resolvedSkills.description: BluOS CLI (blu) for discovery, playback, grouping, and volume.

resolvedSkills.name: camsnap
resolvedSkills.description: Capture frames or clips from RTSP/ONVIF cameras.

resolvedSkills.name: clawflow
resolvedSkills.description: Use when work should span one or more detached tasks but still behave like one job with a single owner context. ClawFlow is the runtime substrate under authoring layers like Lobster, acpx, or plain code. Keep conditional logic in the caller; use ClawFlow for flow identity, waiting, outputs, and user-facing emergence.

resolvedSkills.name: clawflow-inbox-triage
resolvedSkills.description: Example ClawFlow authoring pattern for inbox triage. Use when messages need different treatment based on intent, with some routes notifying immediately, some waiting on outside answers, and others rolling into a later summary.

resolvedSkills.name: clawhub
resolvedSkills.description: Use the ClawHub CLI to search, install, update, and publish agent skills from clawhub.com. Use when you need to fetch new skills on the fly, sync installed skills to latest or a specific version, or publish new/updated skill folders with the npm-installed clawhub CLI.

resolvedSkills.name: coding-agent
resolvedSkills.description: Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Claude Code: use --print --permission-mode bypassPermissions (no PTY). Codex/Pi/OpenCode: pty:true required.

resolvedSkills.name: eightctl
resolvedSkills.description: Control Eight Sleep pods (status, temperature, alarms, schedules).

resolvedSkills.name: gemini
resolvedSkills.description: Gemini CLI for one-shot Q&A, summaries, and generation.

resolvedSkills.name: gh-issues
resolvedSkills.description: Fetch GitHub issues, spawn sub-agents to implement fixes and open PRs, then monitor and address PR review comments. Usage: /gh-issues [owner/repo] [--label bug] [--limit 5] [--milestone v1.0] [--assignee @me] [--fork user/repo] [--watch] [--interval 5] [--reviews-only] [--cron] [--dry-run] [--model glm-5] [--notify-channel -1002381931352]

resolvedSkills.name: gifgrep
resolvedSkills.description: Search GIF providers with CLI/TUI, download results, and extract stills/sheets.

resolvedSkills.name: github
resolvedSkills.description: GitHub operations via `gh` CLI: issues, PRs, CI runs, code review, API queries. Use when: (1) checking PR status or CI, (2) creating/commenting on issues, (3) listing/filtering PRs or issues, (4) viewing run logs. NOT for: complex web UI interactions requiring manual browser flows (use browser tooling when available), bulk operations across many repos (script with gh api), or when gh auth is not configured.

resolvedSkills.name: gog
resolvedSkills.description: Google Workspace CLI for Gmail, Calendar, Drive, Contacts, Sheets, and Docs.

resolvedSkills.name: healthcheck
resolvedSkills.description: Host security hardening and risk-tolerance configuration for OpenClaw deployments. Use when a user asks for security audits, firewall/SSH/update hardening, risk posture, exposure review, OpenClaw cron scheduling for periodic checks, or version status checks on a machine running OpenClaw (laptop, workstation, Pi, VPS).

resolvedSkills.name: himalaya
resolvedSkills.description: CLI to manage emails via IMAP/SMTP. Use `himalaya` to list, read, write, reply, forward, search, and organize emails from the terminal. Supports multiple accounts and message composition with MML (MIME Meta Language).

resolvedSkills.name: imsg
resolvedSkills.description: iMessage/SMS CLI for listing chats, history, and sending messages via Messages.app.

resolvedSkills.name: mcporter
resolvedSkills.description: Use the mcporter CLI to list, configure, auth, and call MCP servers/tools directly (HTTP or stdio), including ad-hoc servers, config edits, and CLI/type generation.

resolvedSkills.name: nano-pdf
resolvedSkills.description: Edit PDFs with natural-language instructions using the nano-pdf CLI.

resolvedSkills.name: node-connect
resolvedSkills.description: Diagnose OpenClaw node connection and pairing failures for Android, iOS, and macOS companion apps. Use when QR/setup code/manual connect fails, local Wi-Fi works but VPS/tailnet does not, or errors mention pairing required, unauthorized, bootstrap token invalid or expired, gateway.bind, gateway.remote.url, Tailscale, or plugins.entries.device-pair.config.publicUrl.

resolvedSkills.description: Work with Obsidian vaults (plain Markdown notes) and automate via obsidian-cli.

resolvedSkills.name: peekaboo

The following skills provide specialized instructions for specific tasks.
Use the read tool to load a skill's file when the task matches its description.
When a skill file references a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory (parent of SKILL.md / dirname of the path) and use that absolute path in tool commands.

resolvedSkills.name: node-connect
resolvedSkills.description: Diagnose OpenClaw node connection and pairing failures for Android, iOS, and macOS companion apps. Use when QR/setup code/manual connect fails, local Wi-Fi works but VPS/tailnet does not, or errors mention pairing required, unauthorized, bootstrap token invalid or expired, gateway.bind, gateway.remote.url, Tailscale, or plugins.entries.device-pair.config.publicUrl.

resolvedSkills.name: obsidian
resolvedSkills.description: Work with Obsidian vaults (plain Markdown notes) and automate via obsidian-cli.

resolvedSkills.name: openhue
resolvedSkills.description: Control Philips Hue lights and scenes via the OpenHue CLI.

resolvedSkills.name: oracle
resolvedSkills.description: Best practices for using the oracle CLI (prompt + file bundling, engines, sessions, and file attachment patterns).

resolvedSkills.name: ordercli
resolvedSkills.description: Foodora-only CLI for checking past orders and active order status (Deliveroo WIP).

resolvedSkills.name: peekaboo
resolvedSkills.description: Capture and automate macOS UI with the Peekaboo CLI.

resolvedSkills.name: skill-creator
resolvedSkills.description: Create, edit, improve, or audit AgentSkills. Use when creating a new skill from scratch or when asked to improve, review, audit, tidy up, or clean up an existing skill or SKILL.md file. Also use when editing or restructuring a skill directory (moving files to references/ or scripts/, removing stale content, validating against the AgentSkills spec). Triggers on phrases like "create a skill", "author a skill", "tidy up a skill", "improve this skill", "review the skill", "clean up the skill", "audit the skill".

resolvedSkills.name: slack
resolvedSkills.description: Use when you need to control Slack from OpenClaw via the slack tool, including reacting to messages or pinning/unpinning items in Slack channels or DMs.

resolvedSkills.name: songsee
resolvedSkills.description: Generate spectrograms and feature-panel visualizations from audio with the songsee CLI.

resolvedSkills.name: sonoscli
resolvedSkills.description: Control Sonos speakers (discover/status/play/volume/group).

resolvedSkills.name: summarize
resolvedSkills.description: Summarize or extract text/transcripts from URLs, podcasts, and local files (great fallback for “transcribe this YouTube/video”).

resolvedSkills.name: things-mac
resolvedSkills.description: Manage Things 3 via the `things` CLI on macOS (add/update projects+todos via URL scheme; read/search/list from the local Things database). Use when a user asks OpenClaw to add a task to Things, list inbox/today/upcoming, search tasks, or inspect projects/areas/tags.

resolvedSkills.name: video-frames
resolvedSkills.description: Extract frames or short clips from videos using ffmpeg.

resolvedSkills.name: wacli
resolvedSkills.description: Send WhatsApp messages to other people or search/sync WhatsApp history via the wacli CLI (not for normal user chats).

resolvedSkills.name: weather
resolvedSkills.description: Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.

skillsSnapshot.prompt:
The following skills provide specialized instructions for specific tasks.
Use the read tool to load a skill's file when the task matches its description.
When a skill file references a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory (parent of SKILL.md / dirname of the path) and use that absolute path in tool commands.

<available_skills>
  <skill>
    <name>1password</name>
    <description>Set up and use 1Password CLI (op). Use when installing the CLI, enabling desktop app integration, signing in (single or multi-account), or reading/injecting/running secrets via op.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/1password/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>apple-notes</name>
    <description>Manage Apple Notes via the `memo` CLI on macOS (create, view, edit, delete, search, move, and export notes). Use when a user asks OpenClaw to add a note, list notes, search notes, or manage note folders.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/apple-notes/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>apple-reminders</name>
    <description>Manage Apple Reminders via remindctl CLI (list, add, edit, complete, delete). Supports lists, date filters, and JSON/plain output.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/apple-reminders/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>bear-notes</name>
    <description>Create, search, and manage Bear notes via grizzly CLI.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/bear-notes/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>blogwatcher</name>
    <description>Monitor blogs and RSS/Atom feeds for updates using the blogwatcher CLI.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/blogwatcher/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>blucli</name>
    <description>BluOS CLI (blu) for discovery, playback, grouping, and volume.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/blucli/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>camsnap</name>
    <description>Capture frames or clips from RTSP/ONVIF cameras.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/camsnap/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>clawflow</name>
    <description>Use when work should span one or more detached tasks but still behave like one job with a single owner context. ClawFlow is the runtime substrate under authoring layers like Lobster, acpx, or plain code. Keep conditional logic in the caller; use ClawFlow for flow identity, waiting, outputs, and user-facing emergence.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/clawflow/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>clawflow-inbox-triage</name>
    <description>Example ClawFlow authoring pattern for inbox triage. Use when messages need different treatment based on intent, with some routes notifying immediately, some waiting on outside answers, and others rolling into a later summary.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/clawflow-inbox-triage/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>clawhub</name>
    <description>Use the ClawHub CLI to search, install, update, and publish agent skills from clawhub.com. Use when you need to fetch new skills on the fly, sync installed skills to latest or a specific version, or publish new/updated skill folders with the npm-installed clawhub CLI.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/clawhub/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>coding-agent</name>
    <description>Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Claude Code: use --print --permission-mode bypassPermissions (no PTY). Codex/Pi/OpenCode: pty:true required.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/coding-agent/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>eightctl</name>
    <description>Control Eight Sleep pods (status, temperature, alarms, schedules).</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/eightctl/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>gemini</name>
    <description>Gemini CLI for one-shot Q&A, summaries, and generation.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/gemini/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>gh-issues</name>
    <description>Fetch GitHub issues, spawn sub-agents to implement fixes and open PRs, then monitor and address PR review comments. Usage: /gh-issues [owner/repo] [--label bug] [--limit 5] [--milestone v1.0] [--assignee @me] [--fork user/repo] [--watch] [--interval 5] [--reviews-only] [--cron] [--dry-run] [--model glm-5] [--notify-channel -1002381931352]</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/gh-issues/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>gifgrep</name>
    <description>Search GIF providers with CLI/TUI, download results, and extract stills/sheets.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/gifgrep/SKILL.md</location>

  <skill>
    <name>github</name>
    <description>GitHub operations via `gh` CLI: issues, PRs, CI runs, code review, API queries. Use when: (1) checking PR status or CI, (2) creating/commenting on issues, (3) listing/filtering PRs or issues, (4) viewing run logs. NOT for: complex web UI interactions requiring manual browser flows (use browser tooling when available), bulk operations across many repos (script with gh api), or when gh auth is not configured.</description>

resolvedSkills.name: 1password
resolvedSkills.description: Set up and use 1Password CLI (op). Use when installing the CLI, enabling desktop app integration, signing in (single or multi-account), or reading/injecting/running secrets via op.

resolvedSkills.name: apple-notes
resolvedSkills.description: Manage Apple Notes via the `memo` CLI on macOS (create, view, edit, delete, search, move, and export notes). Use when a user asks OpenClaw to add a note, list notes, search notes, or manage note folders.

resolvedSkills.name: apple-reminders
resolvedSkills.description: Manage Apple Reminders via remindctl CLI (list, add, edit, complete, delete). Supports lists, date filters, and JSON/plain output.

resolvedSkills.name: bear-notes
resolvedSkills.description: Create, search, and manage Bear notes via grizzly CLI.

resolvedSkills.name: blogwatcher
resolvedSkills.description: Monitor blogs and RSS/Atom feeds for updates using the blogwatcher CLI.

resolvedSkills.name: blucli
resolvedSkills.description: BluOS CLI (blu) for discovery, playback, grouping, and volume.

resolvedSkills.name: camsnap
resolvedSkills.description: Capture frames or clips from RTSP/ONVIF cameras.

resolvedSkills.name: clawflow
resolvedSkills.description: Use when work should span one or more detached tasks but still behave like one job with a single owner context. ClawFlow is the runtime substrate under authoring layers like Lobster, acpx, or plain code. Keep conditional logic in the caller; use ClawFlow for flow identity, waiting, outputs, and user-facing emergence.

resolvedSkills.name: clawflow-inbox-triage
resolvedSkills.description: Example ClawFlow authoring pattern for inbox triage. Use when messages need different treatment based on intent, with some routes notifying immediately, some waiting on outside answers, and others rolling into a later summary.

resolvedSkills.name: clawhub
resolvedSkills.description: Use the ClawHub CLI to search, install, update, and publish agent skills from clawhub.com. Use when you need to fetch new skills on the fly, sync installed skills to latest or a specific version, or publish new/updated skill folders with the npm-installed clawhub CLI.

resolvedSkills.name: coding-agent
resolvedSkills.description: Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Claude Code: use --print --permission-mode bypassPermissions (no PTY). Codex/Pi/OpenCode: pty:true required.

resolvedSkills.name: eightctl
resolvedSkills.description: Control Eight Sleep pods (status, temperature, alarms, schedules).

resolvedSkills.name: gemini
resolvedSkills.description: Gemini CLI for one-shot Q&A, summaries, and generation.

resolvedSkills.name: gh-issues
resolvedSkills.description: Fetch GitHub issues, spawn sub-agents to implement fixes and open PRs, then monitor and address PR review comments. Usage: /gh-issues [owner/repo] [--label bug] [--limit 5] [--milestone v1.0] [--assignee @me] [--fork user/repo] [--watch] [--interval 5] [--reviews-only] [--cron] [--dry-run] [--model glm-5] [--notify-channel -1002381931352]

resolvedSkills.name: gifgrep
resolvedSkills.description: Search GIF providers with CLI/TUI, download results, and extract stills/sheets.

resolvedSkills.name: github
resolvedSkills.description: GitHub operations via `gh` CLI: issues, PRs, CI runs, code review, API queries. Use when: (1) checking PR status or CI, (2) creating/commenting on issues, (3) listing/filtering PRs or issues, (4) viewing run logs. NOT for: complex web UI interactions requiring manual browser flows (use browser tooling when available), bulk operations across many repos (script with gh api), or when gh auth is not configured.

resolvedSkills.name: 1password
resolvedSkills.description: Set up and use 1Password CLI (op). Use when installing the CLI, enabling desktop app integration, signing in (single or multi-account), or reading/injecting/running secrets via op.

resolvedSkills.name: apple-notes
resolvedSkills.description: Manage Apple Notes via the `memo` CLI on macOS (create, view, edit, delete, search, move, and export notes). Use when a user asks OpenClaw to add a note, list notes, search notes, or manage note folders.

resolvedSkills.name: apple-reminders
resolvedSkills.description: Manage Apple Reminders via remindctl CLI (list, add, edit, complete, delete). Supports lists, date filters, and JSON/plain output.

resolvedSkills.name: bear-notes
resolvedSkills.description: Create, search, and manage Bear notes via grizzly CLI.

resolvedSkills.name: blogwatcher
resolvedSkills.description: Monitor blogs and RSS/Atom feeds for updates using the blogwatcher CLI.

resolvedSkills.name: blucli
resolvedSkills.description: BluOS CLI (blu) for discovery, playback, grouping, and volume.

resolvedSkills.name: camsnap
resolvedSkills.description: Capture frames or clips from RTSP/ONVIF cameras.

resolvedSkills.name: clawflow
resolvedSkills.description: Use when work should span one or more detached tasks but still behave like one job with a single owner context. ClawFlow is the runtime substrate under authoring layers like Lobster, acpx, or plain code. Keep conditional logic in the caller; use ClawFlow for flow identity, waiting, outputs, and user-facing emergence.

resolvedSkills.name: clawflow-inbox-triage
resolvedSkills.description: Example ClawFlow authoring pattern for inbox triage. Use when messages need different treatment based on intent, with some routes notifying immediately, some waiting on outside answers, and others rolling into a later summary.

resolvedSkills.name: clawhub
resolvedSkills.description: Use the ClawHub CLI to search, install, update, and publish agent skills from clawhub.com. Use when you need to fetch new skills on the fly, sync installed skills to latest or a specific version, or publish new/updated skill folders with the npm-installed clawhub CLI.

resolvedSkills.name: coding-agent
resolvedSkills.description: Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Claude Code: use --print --permission-mode bypassPermissions (no PTY). Codex/Pi/OpenCode: pty:true required.

resolvedSkills.name: eightctl
resolvedSkills.description: Control Eight Sleep pods (status, temperature, alarms, schedules).

resolvedSkills.name: gemini
resolvedSkills.description: Gemini CLI for one-shot Q&A, summaries, and generation.

resolvedSkills.name: gh-issues
resolvedSkills.description: Fetch GitHub issues, spawn sub-agents to implement fixes and open PRs, then monitor and address PR review comments. Usage: /gh-issues [owner/repo] [--label bug] [--limit 5] [--milestone v1.0] [--assignee @me] [--fork user/repo] [--watch] [--interval 5] [--reviews-only] [--cron] [--dry-run] [--model glm-5] [--notify-channel -1002381931352]

resolvedSkills.name: gifgrep
resolvedSkills.description: Search GIF providers with CLI/TUI, download results, and extract stills/sheets.

resolvedSkills.name: github
resolvedSkills.description: GitHub operations via `gh` CLI: issues, PRs, CI runs, code review, API queries. Use when: (1) checking PR status or CI, (2) creating/commenting on issues, (3) listing/filtering PRs or issues, (4) viewing run logs. NOT for: complex web UI interactions requiring manual browser flows (use browser tooling when available), bulk operations across many repos (script with gh api), or when gh auth is not configured.

resolvedSkills.name: gog
resolvedSkills.description: Google Workspace CLI for Gmail, Calendar, Drive, Contacts, Sheets, and Docs.

resolvedSkills.name: healthcheck
resolvedSkills.description: Host security hardening and risk-tolerance configuration for OpenClaw deployments. Use when a user asks for security audits, firewall/SSH/update hardening, risk posture, exposure review, OpenClaw cron scheduling for periodic checks, or version status checks on a machine running OpenClaw (laptop, workstation, Pi, VPS).

resolvedSkills.name: himalaya
resolvedSkills.description: CLI to manage emails via IMAP/SMTP. Use `himalaya` to list, read, write, reply, forward, search, and organize emails from the terminal. Supports multiple accounts and message composition with MML (MIME Meta Language).

resolvedSkills.name: imsg
resolvedSkills.description: iMessage/SMS CLI for listing chats, history, and sending messages via Messages.app.

resolvedSkills.name: mcporter
resolvedSkills.description: Use the mcporter CLI to list, configure, auth, and call MCP servers/tools directly (HTTP or stdio), including ad-hoc servers, config edits, and CLI/type generation.

resolvedSkills.name: nano-pdf
resolvedSkills.description: Edit PDFs with natural-language instructions using the nano-pdf CLI.

resolvedSkills.name: node-connect
resolvedSkills.description: Diagnose OpenClaw node connection and pairing failures for Android, iOS, and macOS companion apps. Use when QR/setup code/manual connect fails, local Wi-Fi works but VPS/tailnet does not, or errors mention pairing required, unauthorized, bootstrap token invalid or expired, gateway.bind, gateway.remote.url, Tailscale, or plugins.entries.device-pair.config.publicUrl.

NOT for: complex web UI interactions requiring manual browser flows (use browser tooling when available), bulk operations across many repos (script with gh api), or when gh auth is not configured.

resolvedSkills.name: gog
resolvedSkills.description: Google Workspace CLI for Gmail, Calendar, Drive, Contacts, Sheets, and Docs.

resolvedSkills.name: healthcheck
resolvedSkills.description: Host security hardening and risk-tolerance configuration for OpenClaw deployments. Use when a user asks for security audits, firewall/SSH/update hardening, risk posture, exposure review, OpenClaw cron scheduling for periodic checks, or version status checks on a machine running OpenClaw (laptop, workstation, Pi, VPS).

resolvedSkills.name: himalaya
resolvedSkills.description: CLI to manage emails via IMAP/SMTP. Use `himalaya` to list, read, write, reply, forward, search, and organize emails from the terminal. Supports multiple accounts and message composition with MML (MIME Meta Language).

resolvedSkills.name: imsg
resolvedSkills.description: iMessage/SMS CLI for listing chats, history, and sending messages via Messages.app.

resolvedSkills.name: mcporter
resolvedSkills.description: Use the mcporter CLI to list, configure, auth, and call MCP servers/tools directly (HTTP or stdio), including ad-hoc servers, config edits, and CLI/type generation.

resolvedSkills.name: nano-pdf
resolvedSkills.description: Edit PDFs with natural-language instructions using the nano-pdf CLI.

resolvedSkills.name: node-connect
resolvedSkills.description: Diagnose OpenClaw node connection and pairing failures for Android, iOS, and macOS companion apps. Use when QR/setup code/manual connect fails, local Wi-Fi works but VPS/tailnet does not, or errors mention pairing required, unauthorized, bootstrap token invalid or expired, gateway.bind, gateway.remote.url, Tailscale, or plugins.entries.device-pair.config.publicUrl.

resolvedSkills.name: obsidian
resolvedSkills.description: Work with Obsidian vaults (plain Markdown notes) and automate via obsidian-cli.

resolvedSkills.name: openhue
resolvedSkills.description: Control Philips Hue lights and scenes via the OpenHue CLI.

resolvedSkills.name: oracle
resolvedSkills.description: Best practices for using the oracle CLI (prompt + file bundling, engines, sessions, and file attachment patterns).

resolvedSkills.name: ordercli
resolvedSkills.description: Foodora-only CLI for checking past orders and active order status (Deliveroo WIP).

resolvedSkills.name: peekaboo
resolvedSkills.description: Capture and automate macOS UI with the Peekaboo CLI.

resolvedSkills.name: skill-creator
resolvedSkills.description: Create, edit, improve, or audit AgentSkills. Use when creating a new skill from scratch or when asked to improve, review, audit, tidy up, or clean up an existing skill or SKILL.md file. Also use when editing or restructuring a skill directory (moving files to references/ or scripts/, removing stale content, validating against the AgentSkills spec). Triggers on phrases like "create a skill", "author a skill", "tidy up a skill", "improve this skill", "review the skill", "clean up the skill", "audit the skill".

resolvedSkills.name: slack
resolvedSkills.description: Use when you need to control Slack from OpenClaw via the slack tool, including reacting to messages or pinning/unpinning items in Slack channels or DMs.

resolvedSkills.name: songsee
resolvedSkills.description: Generate spectrograms and feature-panel visualizations from audio with the songsee CLI.

resolvedSkills.name: sonoscli
resolvedSkills.description: Control Sonos speakers (discover/status/play/volume/group).

resolvedSkills.name: summarize
resolvedSkills.description: Summarize or extract text/transcripts from URLs, podcasts, and local files (great fallback for “transcribe this YouTube/video”).

resolvedSkills.name: things-mac
resolvedSkills.description: Manage Things 3 via the `things` CLI on macOS (add/update projects+todos via URL scheme; read/search/list from the local Things database). Use when a user asks OpenClaw to add a task to Things, list inbox/today/upcoming, search tasks, or inspect projects/areas/tags.

resolvedSkills.name: video-frames
resolvedSkills.description: Extract frames or short clips from videos using ffmpeg.

resolvedSkills.name: wacli
resolvedSkills.description: Send WhatsApp messages to other people or search/sync WhatsApp history via the wacli CLI (not for normal user chats).

resolvedSkills.name: weather
resolvedSkills.description: Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.

skillsSnapshot.prompt:
The following skills provide specialized instructions for specific tasks.
Use the read tool to load a skill's file when the task matches its description.
When a skill file references a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory (parent of SKILL.md / dirname of the path) and use that absolute path in tool commands.

    <description>Set up and use 1Password CLI (op). Use when installing the CLI, enabling desktop app integration, signing in (single or multi-account), or reading/injecting/running secrets via op.</description>

  </skill>

    <name>clawflow</name>

ClawFlow is the runtime substrate under authoring layers like Lobster, acpx, or plain code. Keep conditional logic in the caller; use ClawFlow for flow identity, waiting, outputs, and user-facing emergence.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/clawflow/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>

    <name>clawflow-inbox-triage</name>

    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/clawhub/SKILL.md</location>

  <skill>
    <name>coding-agent</name>
    <description>Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). resolvedSkills.name: 1password
resolvedSkills.description: Set up and use 1Password CLI (op). Use when installing the CLI, enabling desktop app integration, signing in (single or multi-account), or reading/injecting/running secrets via op.

resolvedSkills.name: apple-notes
resolvedSkills.description: Manage Apple Notes via the `memo` CLI on macOS (create, view, edit, delete, search, move, and export notes). Use when a user asks OpenClaw to add a note, list notes, search notes, or manage note folders.

resolvedSkills.name: apple-reminders
resolvedSkills.description: Manage Apple Reminders via remindctl CLI (list, add, edit, complete, delete). Supports lists, date filters, and JSON/plain output.

resolvedSkills.name: bear-notes
resolvedSkills.description: Create, search, and manage Bear notes via grizzly CLI.

resolvedSkills.name: blogwatcher
resolvedSkills.description: Monitor blogs and RSS/Atom feeds for updates using the blogwatcher CLI.

resolvedSkills.name: blucli
resolvedSkills.description: BluOS CLI (blu) for discovery, playback, grouping, and volume.

resolvedSkills.name: camsnap
resolvedSkills.description: Capture frames or clips from RTSP/ONVIF cameras.

resolvedSkills.name: clawflow
resolvedSkills.description: Use when work should span one or more detached tasks but still behave like one job with a single owner context. ClawFlow is the runtime substrate under authoring layers like Lobster, acpx, or plain code. Keep conditional logic in the caller; use ClawFlow for flow identity, waiting, outputs, and user-facing emergence.

resolvedSkills.name: clawflow-inbox-triage
resolvedSkills.description: Example ClawFlow authoring pattern for inbox triage. Use when messages need different treatment based on intent, with some routes notifying immediately, some waiting on outside answers, and others rolling into a later summary.

resolvedSkills.name: clawhub
resolvedSkills.description: Use the ClawHub CLI to search, install, update, and publish agent skills from clawhub.com. Use when you need to fetch new skills on the fly, sync installed skills to latest or a specific version, or publish new/updated skill folders with the npm-installed clawhub CLI.

resolvedSkills.name: coding-agent
resolvedSkills.description: Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Claude Code: use --print --permission-mode bypassPermissions (no PTY). Codex/Pi/OpenCode: pty:true required.

resolvedSkills.name: eightctl
resolvedSkills.description: Control Eight Sleep pods (status, temperature, alarms, schedules).

resolvedSkills.name: gemini
resolvedSkills.description: Gemini CLI for one-shot Q&A, summaries, and generation.

resolvedSkills.name: gh-issues
resolvedSkills.description: Fetch GitHub issues, spawn sub-agents to implement fixes and open PRs, then monitor and address PR review comments. Use the read tool to load a skill's file when the task matches its description.
When a skill file references a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory (parent of SKILL.md / dirname of the path) and use that absolute path in tool commands.

resolvedSkills.name: 1password

resolvedSkills.name: apple-notes
resolvedSkills.description: Manage Apple Notes via the `memo` CLI on macOS (create, view, edit, delete, search, move, and export notes). Use when a user asks OpenClaw to add a note, list notes, search notes, or manage note folders.

resolvedSkills.name: apple-reminders
resolvedSkills.description: Manage Apple Reminders via remindctl CLI (list, add, edit, complete, delete). Supports lists, date filters, and JSON/plain output.

resolvedSkills.name: bear-notes
resolvedSkills.description: Create, search, and manage Bear notes via grizzly CLI.

resolvedSkills.name: blogwatcher
resolvedSkills.description: Monitor blogs and RSS/Atom feeds for updates using the blogwatcher CLI.

resolvedSkills.name: blucli
resolvedSkills.description: BluOS CLI (blu) for discovery, playback, grouping, and volume.

resolvedSkills.name: camsnap
resolvedSkills.description: Capture frames or clips from RTSP/ONVIF cameras.

resolvedSkills.name: clawflow
resolvedSkills.description: Use when work should span one or more detached tasks but still behave like one job with a single owner context. ClawFlow is the runtime substrate under authoring layers like Lobster, acpx, or plain code. Keep conditional logic in the caller; use ClawFlow for flow identity, waiting, outputs, and user-facing emergence.

resolvedSkills.name: clawflow-inbox-triage
resolvedSkills.description: Example ClawFlow authoring pattern for inbox triage. Use when messages need different treatment based on intent, with some routes notifying immediately, some waiting on outside answers, and others rolling into a later summary.

resolvedSkills.name: clawhub
resolvedSkills.description: Use the ClawHub CLI to search, install, update, and publish agent skills from clawhub.com. Use when you need to fetch new skills on the fly, sync installed skills to latest or a specific version, or publish new/updated skill folders with the npm-installed clawhub CLI.

resolvedSkills.name: coding-agent
resolvedSkills.description: Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Claude Code: use --print --permission-mode bypassPermissions (no PTY). Codex/Pi/OpenCode: pty:true required.

resolvedSkills.name: eightctl
resolvedSkills.description: Control Eight Sleep pods (status, temperature, alarms, schedules).

resolvedSkills.name: gemini
resolvedSkills.description: Gemini CLI for one-shot Q&A, summaries, and generation.

resolvedSkills.name: gh-issues
resolvedSkills.description: Fetch GitHub issues, spawn sub-agents to implement fixes and open PRs, then monitor and address PR review comments. Usage: /gh-issues [owner/repo] [--label bug] [--limit 5] [--milestone v1.0] [--assignee @me] [--fork user/repo] [--watch] [--interval 5] [--reviews-only] [--cron] [--dry-run] [--model glm-5] [--notify-channel -1002381931352]

resolvedSkills.name: gifgrep
resolvedSkills.description: Search GIF providers with CLI/TUI, download results, and extract stills/sheets.

resolvedSkills.name: github
resolvedSkills.description: GitHub operations via `gh` CLI: issues, PRs, CI runs, code review, API queries. Use when: (1) checking PR status or CI, (2) creating/commenting on issues, (3) listing/filtering PRs or issues, (4) viewing run logs. NOT for: complex web UI interactions requiring manual browser flows (use browser tooling when available), bulk operations across many repos (script with gh api), or when gh auth is not configured.

resolvedSkills.name: gog

resolvedSkills.name: healthcheck
resolvedSkills.description: Host security hardening and risk-tolerance configuration for OpenClaw deployments. resolvedSkills.name: himalaya

resolvedSkills.description: Edit PDFs with natural-language instructions using the nano-pdf CLI.

Use the read tool to load a skill's file when the task matches its description.

resolvedSkills.description: Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Claude Code: use --print --permission-mode bypassPermissions (no PTY). Codex/Pi/OpenCode: pty:true required.

resolvedSkills.name: eightctl
resolvedSkills.description: Control Eight Sleep pods (status, temperature, alarms, schedules).

resolvedSkills.name: gemini
resolvedSkills.description: Gemini CLI for one-shot Q&A, summaries, and generation.

resolvedSkills.name: gh-issues
resolvedSkills.description: Fetch GitHub issues, spawn sub-agents to implement fixes and open PRs, then monitor and address PR review comments. Usage: /gh-issues [owner/repo] [--label bug] [--limit 5] [--milestone v1.0] [--assignee @me] [--fork user/repo] [--watch] [--interval 5] [--reviews-only] [--cron] [--dry-run] [--model glm-5] [--notify-channel -1002381931352]

resolvedSkills.name: gifgrep
resolvedSkills.description: Search GIF providers with CLI/TUI, download results, and extract stills/sheets.

resolvedSkills.name: github
resolvedSkills.description: GitHub operations via `gh` CLI: issues, PRs, CI runs, code review, API queries. Use when: (1) checking PR status or CI, (2) creating/commenting on issues, (3) listing/filtering PRs or issues, (4) viewing run logs. NOT for: complex web UI interactions requiring manual browser flows (use browser tooling when available), bulk operations across many repos (script with gh api), or when gh auth is not configured.

resolvedSkills.name: gog
resolvedSkills.description: Google Workspace CLI for Gmail, Calendar, Drive, Contacts, Sheets, and Docs.

resolvedSkills.name: healthcheck
resolvedSkills.description: Host security hardening and risk-tolerance configuration for OpenClaw deployments. Use when a user asks for security audits, firewall/SSH/update hardening, risk posture, exposure review, OpenClaw cron scheduling for periodic checks, or version status checks on a machine running OpenClaw (laptop, workstation, Pi, VPS).

resolvedSkills.name: himalaya
resolvedSkills.description: CLI to manage emails via IMAP/SMTP. Use `himalaya` to list, read, write, reply, forward, search, and organize emails from the terminal. Supports multiple accounts and message composition with MML (MIME Meta Language).

resolvedSkills.name: imsg
resolvedSkills.description: iMessage/SMS CLI for listing chats, history, and sending messages via Messages.app.

resolvedSkills.name: mcporter
resolvedSkills.description: Use the mcporter CLI to list, configure, auth, and call MCP servers/tools directly (HTTP or stdio), including ad-hoc servers, config edits, and CLI/type generation.

resolvedSkills.name: nano-pdf
resolvedSkills.description: Edit PDFs with natural-language instructions using the nano-pdf CLI.

resolvedSkills.name: node-connect
resolvedSkills.description: Diagnose OpenClaw node connection and pairing failures for Android, iOS, and macOS companion apps. Use when QR/setup code/manual connect fails, local Wi-Fi works but VPS/tailnet does not, or errors mention pairing required, unauthorized, bootstrap token invalid or expired, gateway.bind, gateway.remote.url, Tailscale, or plugins.entries.device-pair.config.publicUrl.

resolvedSkills.name: obsidian
resolvedSkills.description: Work with Obsidian vaults (plain Markdown notes) and automate via obsidian-cli.

resolvedSkills.name: openhue
resolvedSkills.description: Control Philips Hue lights and scenes via the OpenHue CLI.

resolvedSkills.name: oracle
resolvedSkills.description: Best practices for using the oracle CLI (prompt + file bundling, engines, sessions, and file attachment patterns).

resolvedSkills.name: ordercli
resolvedSkills.description: Foodora-only CLI for checking past orders and active order status (Deliveroo WIP).

resolvedSkills.name: peekaboo
resolvedSkills.description: Capture and automate macOS UI with the Peekaboo CLI.

resolvedSkills.name: skill-creator
resolvedSkills.description: Create, edit, improve, or audit AgentSkills. Use when creating a new skill from scratch or when asked to improve, review, audit, tidy up, or clean up an existing skill or SKILL.md file. Also use when editing or restructuring a skill directory (moving files to references/ or scripts/, removing stale content, validating against the AgentSkills spec). Triggers on phrases like "create a skill", "author a skill", "tidy up a skill", "improve this skill", "review the skill", "clean up the skill", "audit the skill".

resolvedSkills.name: slack
resolvedSkills.description: Use when you need to control Slack from OpenClaw via the slack tool, including reacting to messages or pinning/unpinning items in Slack channels or DMs.

resolvedSkills.name: songsee
resolvedSkills.description: Generate spectrograms and feature-panel visualizations from audio with the songsee CLI.

resolvedSkills.name: sonoscli
resolvedSkills.description: Control Sonos speakers (discover/status/play/volume/group).

resolvedSkills.name: summarize
resolvedSkills.description: Summarize or extract text/transcripts from URLs, podcasts, and local files (great fallback for “transcribe this YouTube/video”).

resolvedSkills.name: things-mac
resolvedSkills.description: Manage Things 3 via the `things` CLI on macOS (add/update projects+todos via URL scheme; read/search/list from the local Things database). Use when a user asks OpenClaw to add a task to Things, list inbox/today/upcoming, search tasks, or inspect projects/areas/tags.

resolvedSkills.name: video-frames
resolvedSkills.description: Extract frames or short clips from videos using ffmpeg.

resolvedSkills.name: wacli
resolvedSkills.description: Send WhatsApp messages to other people or search/sync WhatsApp history via the wacli CLI (not for normal user chats).

resolvedSkills.name: weather
resolvedSkills.description: Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.

skillsSnapshot.prompt:
The following skills provide specialized instructions for specific tasks.
Use the read tool to load a skill's file when the task matches its description.
When a skill file references a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory (parent of SKILL.md / dirname of the path) and use that absolute path in tool commands.

resolvedSkills.name: things-mac
resolvedSkills.description: Manage Things 3 via the `things` CLI on macOS (add/update projects+todos via URL scheme; read/search/list from the local Things database). Use when a user asks OpenClaw to add a task to Things, list inbox/today/upcoming, search tasks, or inspect projects/areas/tags.

resolvedSkills.name: video-frames
resolvedSkills.description: Extract frames or short clips from videos using ffmpeg.

resolvedSkills.name: wacli
resolvedSkills.description: Send WhatsApp messages to other people or search/sync WhatsApp history via the wacli CLI (not for normal user chats).

resolvedSkills.name: weather
resolvedSkills.description: Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.

skillsSnapshot.prompt:
The following skills provide specialized instructions for specific tasks.
Use the read tool to load a skill's file when the task matches its description.
When a skill file references a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory (parent of SKILL.md / dirname of the path) and use that absolute path in tool commands.

<available_skills>
  <skill>
    <name>1password</name>
    <description>Set up and use 1Password CLI (op). Use when installing the CLI, enabling desktop app integration, signing in (single or multi-account), or reading/injecting/running secrets via op.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/1password/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>apple-notes</name>
    <description>Manage Apple Notes via the `memo` CLI on macOS (create, view, edit, delete, search, move, and export notes). Use when a user asks OpenClaw to add a note, list notes, search notes, or manage note folders.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/apple-notes/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>apple-reminders</name>
    <description>Manage Apple Reminders via remindctl CLI (list, add, edit, complete, delete). Supports lists, date filters, and JSON/plain output.</description>

  </skill>

  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>camsnap</name>
    <description>Capture frames or clips from RTSP/ONVIF cameras.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/camsnap/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>clawflow</name>
    <description>Use when work should span one or more detached tasks but still behave like one job with a single owner context. ClawFlow is the runtime substrate under authoring layers like Lobster, acpx, or plain code. Keep conditional logic in the caller; use ClawFlow for flow identity, waiting, outputs, and user-facing emergence.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/clawflow/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>clawflow-inbox-triage</name>
    <description>Example ClawFlow authoring pattern for inbox triage. Use when messages need different treatment based on intent, with some routes notifying immediately, some waiting on outside answers, and others rolling into a later summary.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/clawflow-inbox-triage/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>clawhub</name>
    <description>Use the ClawHub CLI to search, install, update, and publish agent skills from clawhub.com. Use when you need to fetch new skills on the fly, sync installed skills to latest or a specific version, or publish new/updated skill folders with the npm-installed clawhub CLI.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/clawhub/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>coding-agent</name>
    <description>Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Claude Code: use --print --permission-mode bypassPermissions (no PTY). Codex/Pi/OpenCode: pty:true required.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/coding-agent/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>eightctl</name>
    <description>Control Eight Sleep pods (status, temperature, alarms, schedules).</description>

  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>gemini</name>
    <description>Gemini CLI for one-shot Q&A, summaries, and generation.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/gemini/SKILL.md</location>

  <skill>
    <name>gh-issues</name>
    <description>Fetch GitHub issues, spawn sub-agents to implement fixes and open PRs, then monitor and address PR review comments.     <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/gh-issues/SKILL.md</location>

  <skill>
    <name>gifgrep</name>

    <name>github</name>

NOT for: complex web UI interactions requiring manual browser flows (use browser tooling when available), bulk operations across many repos (script with gh api), or when gh auth is not configured.</description>
